mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate directly reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, increasing present value of equity claims. A sustained downward trend in FEDFUNDS over the preceding 3 months signals monetary easing that produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.55% as of June 2005 indicates accommodative stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which drives aggregate consumption and corporate earnings. Months with PAYEMS growth exceeding 150,000 jobs produce positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 152,383 thousand as of June 2005 indicates robust labor market
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: Months with PAYEMS growth > 150,000 jobs do not produce significantly higher 20-day forward returns than months with lower growth (t-test p > 0.05), or correlation between PAYEMS growth and forward returns is not statistically significant over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) lead consumer spending and construction activity by 2-3 months. A sustained decline in HOUST below 1,200 thousand units signals weakening housing demand and future consumption contraction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1,200 thousand units for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,365 thousand units as of May 2005 remains above threshold
failure_condition: HOUST declines below 1,200 units do not precede negative 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC over 60-day horizon remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) reflects real economic capacity utilization and manufacturing health. When INDPRO growth decelerates below 1.5% annualized over 3 months while UNRATE rises simultaneously, it signals supply-side weakness and demand destruction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth < 1.5% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in same period
regime_hint: late-cycle deceleration with labor market softening
failure_condition: Instances meeting both trigger conditions do not produce negative 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction effect (joint signal) does not outperform either single-series signal alone (Rank IC comparison).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) directly measure consumer spending, the largest component of GDP. Sustained month-over-month RSAFS growth above 0.5% signals robust demand and earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 527,994 million dollars as of May 2005
regime_hint: any regime with positive consumption momentum
failure_condition: Months with RSAFS growth > 0.5% do not produce significantly higher 5-day forward returns than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC over 5-day horizon falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 3.0% year-over-year signals monetary policy tightening pressure and real rate compression concerns. When CPIAUCSL acceleration coincides with FEDFUNDS remaining below 2.5%, it creates a regime of negative real rates that initially supports equity valuations but signals future policy tightening risk, producing negative 60-day returns.
regime_hint: monetary policy transition from easing to tightening with rising inflation
failure_condition: Instances meeting both trigger conditions do not produce negative 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the joint signal does not demonstrate superior predictive power compared to single-series signals (Rank IC comparison).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 4.0% signals labor market deterioration and future earnings pressure. A 0.3 percentage point increase in UNRATE over 3 months predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days due to reduced consumer spending and corporate profit margins.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.5% as of June 2005 remains near cyclical lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with emerging labor market weakness
failure_condition: Instances with UNRATE increases > 0.3pp do not produce negative 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or Rank IC over 20-day horizon remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly reflects aggregate demand strength. When GDPC1 annualized growth decelerates below 2.5% from prior quarter while HOUST simultaneously falls, it signals demand exhaustion and future earnings contraction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.5% AND HOUST declines > 3% quarter-over-quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19,121.112 billion (2017 dollars) as of Q1 2005
fact:HOUST at 1,365 thousand units as of May 2005
regime_hint: late-cycle deceleration with synchronized demand weakness across housing and GDP
failure_condition: Instances meeting both trigger conditions do not produce negative 60-day returns in > 65% of cases, or the interaction effect does not demonstrate superior predictive power compared to either single-series signal alone (Rank IC comparison).
